summary: Iteration 4 — Care AI personality/triage engine e2e verified. Backend: 4/5 new iter4 tests PASSED, 1 skipped (routine scenario didn't finalize within 6 turns — soft skip). Regression: 30/31 backend tests pass (same stale TEST_followup appointment from iter-3, unrelated). Conversation continuity confirmed (no greeting repetition across 5 follow-up turns). Chest-pain+radiation+diaphoresis scenario: urgency='emergency', handoff_doctor includes all required keys (chief_complaint, hpi [3-sentence clinical paragraph], ros, red_flags [cardiac cues], assessment='Acute coronary syndrome suspected', recommendations, confidence='High'). Patient document persists onboarding.care_ai_urgency/handoff/red_flags/summary. GET /api/care-ai/summary/{pid} returns has_summary=false pre-intake and full object post-intake (401 when unauth). Frontend: Consultation page (/patients/{id}/consultation) renders CareAISummaryCard on mount — red EMERGENCY banner, Chief Complaint, HPI, Red Flags with 'high' severity chip, ROS chips, Care AI Assessment, numbered Recommendations, confidence pill. All frontend pages load cleanly (/, /patients, /appointments, /messages, /pharmacy, /laboratory, /analytics, /portal).

minor.issue: Stale TEST_followup appointment lingers from prior iterations with patient_name='Unknown'. No DELETE endpoint exists. Pre-existing from iter-3, not related to Care AI changes.

Mild-headache routine scenario did not emit <SUMMARY> within 6 turns in one run (test was marked SKIPPED rather than FAILED). LLM may require more turns for low-acuity cases, or the system-prompt could encourage closing out routine intakes after 5-6 turns.

issues: Red-flag severity chip uses 'high' (lowercase) pill — looks fine but is slightly less scannable than the EMERGENCY banner; acceptable.

action_items: (Optional) Tune CARE_AI_SYSTEM prompt to finalize routine/low-acuity scenarios within ~5 turns so the <SUMMARY> tag fires consistently (emergency flow already finalizes appropriately).
(Carryover from iter-3) Add DELETE /api/appointments/{id} or /api/admin/cleanup to remove orphan TEST_followup records whose patient_name renders as 'Unknown' in /appointments.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~1403 lines. Strongly recommend splitting into routers/care_ai.py, routers/patients.py, routers/appointments.py, services/llm.py to keep the file under 700 lines.

_careai_chat_call loads db.care_ai_chats history and injects it into the system message each turn — good fix for the Iter-3 LlmChat session-drift bug. Consider capping history to last ~20 turns to avoid prompt bloat on long sessions.

_parse_summary tolerates a missing closing </SUMMARY> tag poorly — if LLM emits '<SUMMARY>...' without a newline before content, the patient could see a stray tag. Add a regex that strips any lingering '<SUMMARY>' token even when JSON parse fails.

GET /api/care-ai/summary/{patient_id} does not check that the caller (if a patient role) owns that patient_id; currently only doctor role is authorized so ok for now — flag for later multi-tenant work.

Care AI finalization on chest-pain scenario returned only 1 red_flag as a single combined string ('crushing chest pain radiating to left arm and jaw with diaphoresis and shortness of breath'). The review request asked for red_flags to 'include cardiac red flags' (plural). Consider prompting the LLM to emit separate red_flag objects per symptom (chest pain, radiation, diaphoresis, SOB) so the UI can show individual severity chips.

success_rate.backend: iter4-specific 4/5 passed + 1 skipped (80% strict, 100% non-skipped); regression 30/31 (97%, same pre-existing stale-appt)
success_rate.frontend: 100% — Consultation page summary card renders all required sections (banner/CC/HPI/red-flags/ROS/assessment/recommendations/confidence); all nav pages load

seed_data_creation: Created 4 TEST_ patients during iter4 run (TEST Summary Empty, TEST Continuity, TEST Emergency, TEST Routine) via POST /api/patients. TEST Emergency has a complete Care AI handoff with urgency='emergency' and is used as a visual verification fixture on /patients/<id>/consultation. No cleanup endpoint exists; records left in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<backend-url> pytest /app/backend/tests/test_care_ai_iter4.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/care_ai_iter4_results.xml`. Key testids: care-ai-summary-card (appears on Consultation page when patient.onboarding.care_ai_handoff exists), care-ai-root, care-ai-chat, care-ai-messages, care-ai-input, care-ai-send (only on /patients/{id}/onboarding, and only while onboarding is not yet complete — completed patients redirect away). Emergency triage produced urgency='emergency' with HPI ≥3 sentences. GET /api/care-ai/summary/{pid} returns {has_summary, urgency, handoff_doctor, summary, red_flags, completed_at}.
rca_of_the_issue: Routine-scenario skip RCA: the CARE_AI_SYSTEM prompt has no explicit guidance on when to finalize low-acuity cases. For chest-pain (emergency), the system correctly recognized red flags after 2-3 turns and emitted <SUMMARY>. For a benign headache with no red flags, GPT-4o keeps asking follow-ups looking for hidden red flags — legitimate clinical caution, but it means 6 turns wasn't always enough to hit <SUMMARY>. Fix: add a rule like 'After 5 turns or when you have chief complaint + onset + severity + associated symptoms + negative red flags, emit <SUMMARY> with urgency=medium/low.' Stale appointment RCA (carryover from iter-3): no DELETE endpoint, /api/seed wipes demo patients but not user-created appointments.
